# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Install system utilities using apt-get
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y \
    build-essential \
    curl \
    git \
    vim \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json
COPY package*.json ./

# Install application dependencies
RUN npm install

# Bundle app source inside Docker image
COPY . .

# Make port 3000 available to the world outside this container
EXPOSE 3000

# Define environment variable
ENV NAME World

# Run app.py when the container launches
CMD ["node", "app.js"]
